priority: Opt-in libssh2 backend with deterministic SSH2 facade
scope: Registered SSH2 functions, facade classes, and common constants
Request-local SSH2 session handles without implicit network access
Request-local SFTP handle modeling attached to sessions
Credential-safe auth failures without logging usernames, passwords, or key passphrases
Deterministic methods/fingerprint shapes for app control flow
Explicit failure for shell, tunnel, forwarding, publickey admin, and backend-required SFTP operations
libssh2-backed ssh2_connect when PHRUST_NET_TESTS and PHRUST_SSH2_LIVE_ENDPOINT exactly match the requested endpoint
libssh2-backed password and public-key file authentication
libssh2-backed ssh2_exec returning a readable stream resource
libssh2-backed ssh2_sftp handle creation, ssh2_scp_recv, ssh2_scp_send, and ssh2_disconnect
non_scope: Host-key known_hosts validation and trust policy management
Interactive shell, tunnels, port forwarding, and publickey admin functions
Full SFTP filesystem mutation/stat/readlink operations beyond backend handle creation
next_step: Promote additional SFTP filesystem operations once the stream wrapper layer can expose ssh2.sftp:// handles.
